steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: In an isosceles triangle, exactly two angles are equal. Angle A = 50°. Three cases to check: (1) A = B, (2) A = C, (3) B = C.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Case 1: A = B = 50°, so C = 180° - 50° - 50° = 80°. Valid triangle. B = 50°. Case 2: A = C = 50°, so B = 180° - 50° - 50° = 80°. Valid. B = 80°. Case 3: B = C, so 50° + 2B = 180°, giving B = 65°. Valid.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Possible values of angle B are 50°, 65°, and 80°. B = 100° is not achievable: if B = 100°, then A + B = 150°, C = 30°. The angles 50°, 100°, 30° have no two equal — not isosceles.
